response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with over two decades of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application development. The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture expertise, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but they are far from the silicon-level performance architecture this principal role demands.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience, easily satisfying the BS + 8 years path
Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms), plus recent AI-agent tooling work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture — a fundamental domain mismatch for a principal hardware-adjacent IC role

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator / deep-learning workload optimization background
